findings.title: ruleSignalMarkers sort comparator is unsound for mixed string/number times
findings.suggested_fix: After calling toET, the time is always a number (for intraday) or a date string (for daily). Comparing numbers and strings with < / > in JS produces coercions that silently mis-sort daily bars (e.g. '2024-03-01' < 1709251200 coerces to NaN-based comparison, which is always false). The mainMarkers sort in the merge (line 611) has the same issue. Use an explicit numeric coercion: `(a, b) => Number(a.time) - Number(b.time)` for intraday-only datasets, or a consistent typed comparator that handles both. Currently this is obscured by the double `as any` on the time field, which prevents the compiler from flagging the heterogeneous union.

findings.title: mainMarkersPluginRef typed as useRef<any> — createSeriesMarkers plugin interface is fully typed in lw-charts v5
findings.suggested_fix: lightweight-charts v5 exports ISeriesMarkersPluginApi (or similar). Type the ref as `useRef<ReturnType<typeof createSeriesMarkers> | null>(null)`. This eliminates the two `as any` casts on lines 615 and 617 and lets the compiler catch future API surface changes (e.g. if setMarkers is renamed).

findings.title: Rule.params typed as Record<string, any> — widens all downstream indicator param access to any
findings.suggested_fix: This is a pre-existing issue but B4 adds new code paths (ruleSignalMarkers) that flow Rule values all the way into chart rendering without any narrowing. Consider a discriminated union keyed on indicator, or at minimum `Record<string, number | string | boolean | undefined>` to prevent structural any bleed.

findings.title: rule_index color assignment is index-within-its-own-side but may collide between sides
findings.suggested_fix: The backend emits rule_index as the per-side index (buy rules enumerated separately from sell rules — see backtest.py line 433-436). A buy rule at index 0 and a sell rule at index 0 will therefore get the same color (RULE_SIGNAL_COLORS[0]). The legend also shows both with the same dot. If the intent is unique colors per signal track, the frontend needs to assign a de-duplicated color slot, e.g. using the array position in ruleSignals rather than rs.rule_index.

findings.title: Merged marker array cast to `as any[]` then `as any` before createSeriesMarkers — removes all type checking on marker shape
findings.suggested_fix: ruleSignalMarkers pushes objects with a `size` field (line 222) which is not part of the SeriesMarker type in lightweight-charts v5. Rather than suppressing the error with `as any`, remove the unknown `size` field (the property is not honored by the library) or confirm the field name with the lw-charts v5 API. Typing the local objects correctly would catch the mismatch at compile time.

findings.title: Eye toggle is a raw emoji button with no accessible label beyond title attribute
findings.suggested_fix: The mute and NOT buttons in the same component use lucide-react icons with consistent sizing. The eye toggle uses a raw 👁 emoji at fontSize 14. This is inconsistent with the rest of the row and the emoji renders at different sizes across platforms. Replace with `<Eye size={13} />` from lucide-react and add `aria-pressed={visualize}` to make the toggle state machine-readable. This is a P3 because it is functionally correct.

residual_risks.title: visualize flag survives into SavedStrategy serialization but is not declared on SavedStrategy
residual_risks.detail: Rule.visualize is saved inside buyRules/sellRules arrays when a strategy is persisted to localStorage (SavedStrategy). That is fine structurally — Rule[] carries the field. But if a saved strategy is loaded and re-submitted, the backend receives visualize:true for rules the user may not have consciously opted in to, causing extra signal computation on every rerun. Clarify whether visualize should be stripped before persistence or whether this is the intended behavior.

residual_risks.title: eval_rule (singular) is called in the backtest loop, not eval_rules — negated flag is not applied to visualized signals
residual_risks.detail: The backtest route calls eval_rule(rule, indicators, i) directly for signal visualization (backtest.py line 441). eval_rules() applies the negated flag (signal_engine.py line 464). eval_rule() does not. So a rule with negated=true will show raw (un-negated) signals on the chart, even though the strategy treats it as negated. This is a backend issue but the TypeScript types surface it: Rule.negated is optional and flows from the frontend unchanged.

testing_gaps.title: No unit tests for ruleSignalMarkers memoization or sort correctness
testing_gaps.detail: The sort comparator on mixed-type times (string vs number after toET) is the highest-risk logic path added in this diff and has no test coverage. A unit test with a daily interval dataset (date strings) alongside an intraday dataset (unix numbers) would catch the comparison coercion bug without needing the full chart to render.

testing_gaps.title: No test that rule_index color slot de-duplication works across buy/sell sides
testing_gaps.detail: The color collision between buy rule index 0 and sell rule index 0 described in the findings is not exercised by any visible test. A snapshot or DOM test that renders the legend with one buy rule and one sell rule, both visualized, would catch duplicate color swatches.
